Apply iOS-style visual design system

Typography:
- Font stack: -apple-system, SF Pro Text/Display, Helvetica Neue
- Base size 17px / regular, letter-spacing -0.022em
- Title 22px/600, subtitle 19px/600, base 17px/400,
  secondary 15px/400, captions 13px/400

Color system (iOS palette):
- Background: #f2f2f7 (iOS grouped bg)
- Text primary: #1c1c1e, secondary: #636366, muted: #8e8e93
- Borders: rgba(60,60,67,0.12)
- Alert red: #ff3b30, orange: #ff9500, green: #34c759

Shadows — very subtle, iOS-style:
- Header: 0.5px hairline separator
- Cards: soft 4/28px layered shadow
- Button: warm yellow glow at lower opacity
- Nav: 0.5px hairline top border

Components:
- Bottom nav: iOS tab bar with blur + dot indicator
- Status chip, heatmap toggle, risk legend: blur backdrop
- Toast: semi-transparent dark blur pill (iOS HUD style)
- Alert button: slightly larger (84px), softer shadow
- Panels/cards: no border sides, rounded bottom only
- Profile hero: hairline separator instead of thick yellow bar
- Stat cards: soft shadow, lighter font weights
- Auth: larger inputs (17px), rounded card (20px)
- Bottom nav active: yellow dot instead of bottom bar

commit.message:
Drug delivery plug payment system, gun reticle, and zone detection fixes

- Add two-phase drug delivery: after dropping, player must bring the plug his cut (40%) to a random location before receiving their share; server-side pending payout prevents bypassing
- Fix plug payment never completing due to missing plugCut in server config (nil arithmetic error)
- Fix delivery and plug zones triggering inconsistently by switching from 3D to 2D distance checks and using Wait(0) per-frame polling when inside the zone so keypresses are never missed
- Lower checkpoint ring Z by 1.0 so rings render at ground level instead of floating
- Enable gun reticle by removing HUD component 14 from disabled list

fix: use try_from instead of as u16 for path length comparison (#580)

path.to_vec().len() as u16 would silently truncate paths with >65535
segments. Changed to u16::try_from().unwrap_or(u16::MAX) which safely
handles the (practically impossible) overflow case.

Tier 2 upgrades: regime classifier, adaptive stops, correlation limits, earnings calendar

Regime Classifier (core/regime_classifier.py):
- 5 market regimes: TRENDING_UP/DOWN, RANGING, HIGH_VOLATILITY, LOW_VOLATILITY
- Signal voting from ADX, ATR ratio, Bollinger bandwidth, SMA slope, RSI
- Portfolio-level classification with regime agreement score
- New ADX + SMA series indicators in tools/technical_indicators.py
- /api/regime dashboard endpoint

Adaptive Stops:
- Trailing stops adapt to regime (trending=0.8x tighter, volatile=2.0x wider)
- Initial stops adapt to regime (volatile=1.3x wider, trending=0.9x tighter)
- Configurable multiplier maps in risk_params.json

Correlation-Based Position Limits:
- Real pairwise correlation check in RiskManager.validate_order()
- Rejects orders when |correlation| > max_correlation with any held position
- Portfolio-level average correlation warning
- 1-hour correlation cache to avoid redundant API calls

Earnings Calendar (core/earnings_calendar.py):
- Hardcoded Q1-Q4 2026 dates for AAPL, NVDA, TSLA, AMZN, META
- Position size halved when earnings within 3 days
- /api/earnings dashboard endpoint

64 new tests (409 total), all passing.

commit.message:
fix: tenant isolation — close cross-tenant content leakage across all public routes

SECURITY: Multiple tenant boundary violations allowed content from one
tenant (e.g. Clearwater Cruisin) to appear on other tenants' pages or
the platform home page.

Root causes fixed:
- queryPageBySlug dropped tenant filter when tenantId was undefined,
  returning pages from ANY tenant (overrideAccess: true bypassed ACL)
- Layout fell back to fetchTenantByDomain which always returns a tenant
  via DEFAULT_TENANT_SLUG, even for platform domains
- detectTenantFromHostname returned 'default' for localhost instead of
  null (platform context)
- All public routes (posts, events, shop, products) had explicit
  "fallback to default tenant" that forced a tenant context where none
  should exist
- adminOrPublishedWithTenantScope returned publishedOnly with NO tenant
  filter when no tenant context, leaking all tenants via REST API

New utilities:
- buildTenantFilter.ts — always returns an explicit tenant WHERE clause
  (either equals:tenantId or exists:false for platform context)
- resolveTenantFromHeaders.ts — shared tenant resolution replacing
  duplicated 20-line blocks across 7 route files

Files fixed: [slug]/page.tsx, posts/page.tsx, posts/[slug]/page.tsx,
posts/page/[pageNumber]/page.tsx, events/page.tsx, events/[slug]/page.tsx,
shop/page.tsx, products/[slug]/page.tsx, layout.tsx, detectTenant.ts,
adminOrPublishedWithTenantScope.ts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve Bootstrap failed error 5 on reinstall

Root cause: watchdog/sentinel SIGTERM handlers fire synchronous network
alerts (10-20s blocking) before exiting. The install script's sleep 2
is far too short — the old processes are still alive and registered in
launchd when bootstrap is called, causing EIO (error 5).

Fix:
- Send SIGKILL (not SIGTERM) during install cleanup — this is a known
  reinstall, not a tamper event
- Poll until processes are actually dead (max 10s) instead of fixed sleep
- Use both legacy launchctl unload AND modern bootout for cleanup
- Fall back from bootstrap to legacy load -w if bootstrap fails
  (more compatible with osascript admin context on some macOS versions)
- Apply same fix to both install_system.sh (DMG path) and install.sh
  (terminal path)
- Bump VERSION to 18

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: propagate IO errors in Visualize for KeyInfoPath (#581)

Replaced .unwrap() calls on fallible Write operations with ? operator
to properly propagate IO errors instead of panicking.

feat: add independent sandbox architecture for provider comparison (#53)

* feat: add independent sandbox architecture for provider comparison

- Add app/agents directory with ApplicationAgent and SandboxDemoAgent
- Add /api/sandboxes/compare endpoint for multi-provider comparison
- Each provider gets its own independent sandbox session
- Add pessimistic locking to SandboxSession#record_run! to prevent race conditions
- Update SandboxRunJob with ActiveAgent integration and fallback
- Update SandboxRunner.jsx to use new compare endpoint

This architecture ensures each provider comparison runs in complete isolation,
eliminating race conditions and enabling true ephemeral container deployment.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: use single sandbox with multiple generation jobs

Changes the compare architecture from separate sandboxes per provider
to a single shared sandbox with parallel generation jobs:

- Update /api/sandboxes/compare to accept sandbox_id and reuse existing sandbox
- Spawn separate SandboxRunJob for each provider in the same sandbox
- Frontend passes existing sandbox_id to compare endpoint
- Poll single sandbox for all run results with run_id matching

This enables single ephemeral container per user with multiple
ActiveAgent GenerationJobs running in parallel.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add ActionCable streaming for real-time provider comparison

- Add useActionCable React hook for WebSocket subscriptions
- Update SandboxRunJob to broadcast run_started, run_complete, run_error events
- Allow anonymous ActionCable connections for free-tier sandbox users
- Update SandboxRunner to handle real-time streaming updates
- Install @rails/actioncable npm package

Results now stream in as each provider completes, showing tokens and duration
for side-by-side comparison across Anthropic, OpenAI, and Ollama.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Create Ragents RubyGem using Ractor pattern and benchmark async Ruby for LLM operations (#54)

* feat: initial release of ragents gem with Ractor-based AI agents

Introduces a pure Ruby gem designed for Ruby 4.x using the Ractor pattern
for parallel agentic workflows. Implements immutable message passing
via `Data.define` to achieve zero-copy communication between Ractors.

Key features:
- Ractor-based Agent and Supervisor orchestration for true parallelism.
- Context management with frozen snapshots for safe object passing.
- Support for agent-as-a-tool calls and MCP interactions.
- Built-in benchmarks comparing Ractors, Threads, and Async fibers.
- CLI tool `bin/bench` for performance analysis and dashboard integration.
- Native support for OpenAI, Anthropic, and Mock providers.

Co-authored-by: Claude Code <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add ragents gem source under ragents/

Pure Ruby gem implementing Ractor-based AI agent orchestration targeting
Ruby 4.x. Included here for testing while the standalone gem repo is
being set up at activeagents/ragents on RubyGems.org.

Gem structure:
- ragents/lib/           Core library (messages, context, tools, providers, ractors)
- ragents/lib/ragents/cli/  Full TUI (ANSI, Braille spinner, slash commands)
- ragents/bin/ragents    Interactive Ractor agent CLI
- ragents/bin/bench      Benchmark runner (posts results to /api/benchmarks)
- ragents/sig/           RBS type signatures
- ragents/test/          Unit tests for all core components
- ragents/ragents.gemspec requires Ruby >= 4.0.0, pure stdlib only

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(ragents): Charm-inspired TUI aesthetic

Redesigns the CLI TUI to match the Charm (charm.sh) aesthetic in pure
Ruby — no Go binaries, no native extensions, stdlib only.

Visual changes:
- Rounded box borders (╭─╮╰─╯) on header, message bubbles, input box
- Charm brand gradient (pink #FF63BA → lavender #868EFF) on ⚡ ragents wordmark
  via true-color (24-bit RGB) ANSI sequences
- Lipgloss-inspired colour palette: pink, lavender, peach, teal, sky, lime, rose
- User / Agent / Error messages rendered in titled rounded boxes
- Tool calls: ◆ name(args)  /  Tool results: ✔ or ✖ with muted preview
- System messages use horizontal rules with centered label
- Pill badges for tool count and token count in the header
- Input box is a full rounded border (matches Charm's textinput component)

Spinner changes:
- Frames: ⣾⣽⣻⢿⡿⣟⣯⣷  (Charm bubbletea spinner.Dot style, 80ms)
- Label cycles through pink→lavender gradient matching the header brand

* feat: Add SimulatedProvider and AsyncSimulatedProvider for benchmarking

- AsyncSimulatedProvider: Fiber-scheduler compatible for Async gem

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: Expand benchmark to 6 concurrency strategies

Strategies now include:
1. Sequential (baseline)
2. Threads (unbounded)
3. Thread Pool (bounded by CPU cores)
4. Ractors (unbounded)
5. Ractor Pool (bounded by CPU cores)
6. Async Fibers (cooperative scheduling)

- Add async, async-http, falcon, async-job gems
- Thread Pool uses producer-consumer queue pattern
- Async Fibers properly yield during I/O simulation
- Results posted to ActiveAgents dashboard

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: Add 6-strategy benchmark report

Documents Ruby 4.0 concurrency benchmark results:
- Threads/Ractors (unbounded): ~175-178 req/s, 18-19x speedup
- Thread/Ractor Pools (bounded): ~94 req/s, 10x speedup
- Async Fibers: 129 req/s, 13.7x speedup

Includes recommendations by workload type and Ruby 4.0 migration notes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: Add --pool-scaling flag for sandbox resource optimization

Analyzes throughput across different pool sizes to find optimal
executor count based on sandbox CPU/RAM allocation.

Example output:
  Pool= 1 →  2127ms |  9.4 req/s | sequential
  Pool= 5 →   442ms | 45.3 req/s | half cores
  Pool=10 →   237ms | 84.3 req/s | = cores
  Pool=20 →   158ms | 126.6 req/s | 2x cores (optimal)

Key insight: I/O-bound LLM workloads can use 2x cores since
threads spend most time waiting on network I/O.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

fix: move logo to src/assets for Vite content-hashing

Static files in public/ have no content hash so browsers cache them
indefinitely. Moving to src/assets/ makes Vite hash the filename
(logo-BhRNkWZv.png) on every build, guaranteeing cache-busting.

Also switched landing hero from rounded-full/object-cover to
object-contain so the full bronze ball renders without dark edge clipping.

Prove lemma79_3a_conjugacy_of_subgroups (Lemma 79.3a, $242 bounty)

Both directions of H0 = {alpha * h * inv(alpha) | h in H1} proved via
path composition decomposition and group algebra in pi1(B,b0).

- Rate limiter prunes stale IPs to prevent memory growth

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Migrate to npm workspaces, add silent WebSocket reconnect, workflow shutdown holds, and fix OAuth origin

Phase 1: Remove nx/pnpm — migrate to npm workspaces
- Replace pnpm-workspace.yaml and nx.json with npm workspaces in root package.json
- Update all workspace:* dependencies to * across 8 packages
- Update build scripts to use npm ci instead of pnpm install
- Update CLAUDE.md documentation for npm-based workflow

Phase 2: Prevent auto-shutdown during workflow actions
- Add holdShutdown()/releaseShutdown() to ConnectionManager with 30min safety timer
- Wire onExecStart/onExecEnd callbacks through WorkflowManager to ConnectionManager
- Prevents EC2 instance stop during long-running workflow commands (npm install, cdk deploy)

Phase 3: Silent WebSocket reconnects with 5s grace period
- Add 'reconnecting' connection state with 5s grace before showing overlay
- Buffer terminal input during reconnect, flush on restore
- Remove legacy fallback HTTP command input
- Keep ConnectionBadge showing green during silent reconnect

Auth fix: Use window.location.origin for OAuth redirect URI
- Frontend derives redirect URI from current origin instead of hardcoded domain
- Add CloudFront distribution domain to Cognito callback URLs in CDK
- Fixes InvalidOriginException when accessing via *.cloudfront.net domain

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix dataset download: use libero_100 (contains LIBERO-10 tasks)

libero_10 is not a valid --datasets argument; libero_100 is the correct
dataset name that contains the 10 tasks used in the LIBERO-10 benchmark.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
test(security): add route auth coverage test — default-deny enforcement

Adds a meta-test that acts as a permanent guard against unauthenticated
access to protected endpoints (the class of bug in C1).

Every /api/ endpoint must either:
  (A) appear in PUBLIC_ENDPOINTS with a documented reason, or
  (B) return 401 when called without a token.

Any new route that lacks an auth guard will fail this test before
reaching production. POST /api/auth/register is permanently in the
sample list as a C1 regression guard.

19 tests passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: detect SSL cert errors and guide users (firewall/MITM)

Detect certificate interception (Fortinet, Zscaler, etc.) in the web
setup wizard, CLI setup, CLI register, and postinstall script. Show
actionable guidance: try a different network, whitelist cluude.ai, or
use CLI fallback.

commit.message:
feat(passkey): multi-step onboarding with mainnet+testnet provisioning

- Split passkey creation and account provisioning into separate steps
- New `/wallet/provision-start` endpoint fires Lilico API for both
  mainnet and testnet in parallel, saves txIds, returns immediately
- New `/wallet/provision-save` endpoint for client to persist addresses
  after polling confirms sealed tx
- Frontend modal transforms from pitch → creating → status card with
  live polling per network, boring-avatar for addresses
- Add flow_address_testnet, provision_tx_mainnet, provision_tx_testnet
  columns to passkey_credentials
- usePasskeyWallet: createPasskey, provisionAccounts, pollProvisionTx,
  saveProvisionedAddress as separate composable functions
